data.title: SA Asks: What's next for Intel after the Altera deal?
data.description:
On Monday, Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) announced it was selling a 51% stake in its Altera programmable chip business to private equity firm Salt Lake for around $4.5B, its first major deal since Lip-Bu Tan was appointed CEO of the beleaguered chipmaker in March.

The deal comes amid reports that Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) is also in the midst of negotiating a potential joint venture for its foundry business with Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and may be interested in selling at least part of its stake in Mobileye (MBLY).

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Jonathan Weber and The Asian Investor what they thought might be next for the US chipmaker.

Jonathan Weber: Intel (INTC) will report its Q4 earnings soon -- while no one expects strong results, many investors and Wall Street will be eager for Intel's guidance. I expect that shares will be volatile following the earnings release, as there is potential for negative or positive surprises. 

Beyond that, the planned joint venture with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) could be quite important for Intel (INTC). If the two companies follow through on that, Intel (INTC) could benefit massively from TSM's (TSM) expertise in managing fabs efficiently. Capital expenditure requirements for cash-strapped Intel (INTC) could decline as well. Depending on how this JV will be structured (and factoring in that there is no guarantee of it happening), this may turn out to be a big win for Intel (INTC). 

Due to its weak profitability and hurting balance sheet, Intel (INTC) is by far not a low-risk stock; thus, I believe that it may be a good idea to only hold INTC as part of a basket of semiconductor stocks, if one is interested in owning INTC at all. 

The Asian Investor: Intel (INTC) is making progress in terms of simplifying its portfolio amid ongoing restructuring challenges. The chipmaker just announced that it is selling more than half (51%) of its programmable chip unit Altera, which is part of Intel’s non-core business, to a strategic investor. Intel is set to receive $4.46B for its majority stake, which represents a decline of 48% compared to the $16.7 billion the chipmaker paid for Altera in 2015.  

Going forward, similarly bold measures may follow. Intel could be eyeing to also sell its Mobileye Global (MBLY) business, which deals with the development of autonomous driving technology. In addition, Intel (INTC) could be looking to bring in a strategic partner to its loss-making foundry unit. 

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) recently announced a $100B investment into U.S. chip making plants and was rumored to being interested in running Intel’s foundry as well. In addition to the Altera deal, I believe investors are likely going to see more drastic actions with regard to Intel’s (INTC) non-core operating portfolio in 2025.

data.title: Starbucks sets a new dress code for baristas
data.description:
Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) is creating a dress code for baristas, with a goal to make its green aprons stand out and create a sense of familiarity for customers.

Beginning on May 12, the dress code in all stores will focus on simplified color options that the company says will let the iconic green aprons shine and create a warmer atmosphere at stores across North America.

The more defined color palette includes any solid black short and long-sleeved crewneck, collared, or button-up shirts and any shade of khaki, black, or blue denim bottoms. Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) is making a new line of company branded t-shirts available to partners, who will receive two at no cost. 

"By updating our dress code, we can deliver a more consistent coffeehouse experience that will also bring simpler and clearer guidance to our partners, which means they can focus on what matters most, crafting great beverages and fostering connections with customers," read part of a Starbucks post on the new policy.

The decision has been attacked by Starbucks Workers United, which is a labor union that has organized workers at more than 550 of Starbucks' (SBUX) company-owned U.S. stores. The union said it opposes any changes to the dress code until bargaining concludes, and a labor agreement is reached.

"Starbucks’ top priority should be finalizing fair contracts with union baristas so we have the staffing, guaranteed hours, and support we need to do our jobs. Workers United has written to Starbucks demanding no dress code change be implemented at union-represented stores until bargaining concludes," stated union bargaining delegate Jasmine Leli.

Starbucks (SBUX) CEO Brian Niccol has initiated a number of dramatic changes in store operations in order for the chain to recapture its coffeehouse vibe.

Shares of Starbucks (SBUX) are down 7.8% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: UnitedHealth among top picks as Baird says managed care is a “tariff safe haven”
data.description:
Baird highlighted UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) and Alignment Healthcare (NASDAQ:ALHC) as its top two safe haven picks, arguing that managed care firms can withstand the effects of the Trump administration’s tariffs.

“The story is simple,” the analyst Michael Ha wrote, arguing that given the sector’s domestic earnings exposure and its focus on Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug (MAPD) and Medicare Part D prescription drug plans, managed care remains largely immune from tariff threats.

“We expect companies to outperform 1Q, but we do not expect guidance raises as we believe companies will maintain 2025G conservatism/cushion given potential Part D and macro headwinds that may evolve later this year," Ha added.

Baird rates UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) and Alignment Healthcare (NASDAQ:ALHC) with Outperform recommendations, with the former’s price target set to $640 and the latter’s target raised to $22 from $17 per share.

Ha issued a Neutral rating on their rival Humana (HUM) but raised its price target to $300 from $270, noting his plans to make the Kentucky-based firm his top pick subject to a positive outcome from its litigation related to its 2026 Star ratings and potentially positive 2027 ratings.

CVS Health (CVS) also faced a Neutral rating with its price target raised to $71 from $51 per share given its earnings potential. “Our multiple reflects a 1x discount on CVS' peak historical five-year P/E, which we believe appropriately reflects our view on CVS' potential embedded earnings power and a slight discount to account for our caution over the next 1–2 quarters,” the analyst wrote.

Despite an Outperform rating, Baird trimmed its price target on Cigna (CI) to $362 from $388, arguing that CI is most exposed to rising unemployment in the event of a recession and citing the company’s lack of exposure to government insurance and hence policy headwinds.

Meanwhile, Ha downgraded Medicaid-exposed managed care firms Molina (MOH), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV), and Centene (CNC) to Neutral from Outperform due to policy risk while adjusting their price targets to $375, $529, and $69 from $331, $625, and $71, respectively.

Citing the same policy overhang, Baird downgraded hospital stocks Tenet Healthcare (THC), Universal Health Services (UHS), and HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) to Neutral from Outperform, with their price targets set to $137, $224, and $336 from $153, $274, and $396, respectively.

“While fundamentals are fine, we are downgrading as we see minimal incremental buyers in these names until policy overhang clears,” the analyst wrote.

data.title: Ameresco cut at Baird on uncertain outlook, Itron added as Bullish Fresh Pick
data.description:
Ameresco (NYSE:AMRC) -4.3% in Tuesday's trading as Baird downgrades shares of the energy infrastructure company to Neutral from Outperform with a $13 price target, slashed from $25, after Q4 results beat estimates for all key metrics, but earnings guidance that was well below consensus will weigh on shares in the near term.

Cost overruns were a negative to Q4 gross margins, and management discussed project pauses due to uncertainty in the federal government, which represented 20% of revenue in 2024, and while Ameresco (NYSE:AMRC) has become a more diverse business with respect to both its backlog and operating energy assets, the uncertainty may create an overhang on the stock, Baird analyst Ben Kallo says.

On the other hand, Baird adds Itron (NASDAQ:ITRI) as a Bullish Fresh Pick, saying an increasingly predictable business as revenue has shifted towards Networks and Outcomes, as well as a robust backlog with relatively a stable customer base of utilities gives increased confidence in the company's ability to beat estimates despite macro uncertainty.

Baird continues to like MP Materials (MP) and Bloom Energy (BE) as its best ideas for 2025 among alternative energy names; MP looks like a winner as rare earths have taken center stage in the geopolitical conflict between the U.S. and China, and deals similar in size and structure to that with AEP could be a major catalyst for Bloom, and the time-to-power proposition remains strong given several companies seeking grid power with limited available supply.

data.title: Kuros Bioscience reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Kuros Bioscience press release (OTCPK:CSBTF): Q1 Total Medical Device sales rose by 82% to USD 28.8 million (Q1 2024: USD 15.8 million) with monthly revenue surpassing USD 10.0 million for the first time
  • Total Group EBITDA reached USD 2.0 million (Q1 2024: EBITDA of USD 1.2 million)
  • Total Group adjusted EBITDA amounted to USD 3.3 million, equaling a margin of 11.6%.

data.title: CoreWeave brings thousands of Nvidia Grace Blackwell GPUs online
data.description:
CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) has brought thousands of Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) Grace Blackwell graphic processing units online, becoming the first cloud provider to make the new GPUs generally available at scale, the companies announced today.

Despite the launch, CoreWeave, a data center cloud company that held its initial public offering late last month, was down 7% by afternoon trading on Tuesday. Nvidia edged up 1.4%.

Artificial intelligence companies such as Cohere, IBM (NYSE:IBM) and Mistral AI are already utilizing the stockpile of Nvidia GB200 NVL72 systems. Canada-based Cohere, which develops large language models for enterprises, indicated it is experiencing three times the performance in training for LLMs over Nvidia's previous-generation Hopper GPUs.

"This unlocks incredible performance efficiency across our stack — from our vertically integrated North application running on a single Blackwell GPU to scaling training jobs across thousands of them," said Autumn Moulder, vice president of engineering at Cohere. 

Meanwhile, IBM is training its next generation of enterprise-ready Granite AI models on the Grace Blackwell racks.

"This collaboration with CoreWeave will augment IBM’s capabilities to help build advanced, high-performance and cost-efficient models for powering enterprise and agentic AI applications with IBM watsonx," said Sriram Raghavan, vice president of AI at IBM Research. 

Paris-based Mistral AI is also one of the first customers to train and deploy models on the new system.

"Right out of the box and without any further optimizations, we saw a 2x improvement in performance for dense model training," said Thimothee Lacroix, cofounder and chief technology officer at Mistral AI.

"CoreWeave is built to move faster – and time and again, we've proven it by being first to operationalize the most advanced systems at scale," said CoreWeave CEO and co-founder Michael Intrator.

data.title: KKR close to purchase of German IT firm Datagroup - Bloomberg
data.description:
Private equity firm KKR (NYSE:KKR) is nearing a purchase of German IT services company Datagroup SE. 

The PE shop is in advanced talks to make a bid for Datagroup and an announcement may occur as early as this week, according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

Talks are ongoing and a deal could be delayed or fall apart, according to the report. Datagroup has a market cap of 340.2 million euros. 

KKR declined to comment to Bloomberg, while Datagroup wasn't immediately available to comment.

KKR (NYSE:KKR) on Monday agreed to buy post-trade services provider OSTTRA for an enterprise value of $3.1 billion.

data.title: Koil Energy targets sustained growth with 2025 strategy and subsea expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Koil Energy Solutions (KLNG) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Erik Wiik highlighted a significant revenue increase to $6 million in Q4, marking a 14% sequential rise and a 48% year-over-year improvement. Full-year 2024 revenue reached $23 million, a 48% increase from 2023, driven by doubling fixed-price projects.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the year was reported as $3.5 million, a historic profitability record, compared to a prior-year loss. Free cash flow of $1.4 million and a year-end cash balance of $3.4 million were noted as supporting liquidity.
  • Wiik emphasized strategic market growth, including securing a contract for subsea distribution equipment in West Africa and a Bend Stiffener Latcher contract in a new region. Koil also established a presence in Brazil with a new manufacturing and service support facility.
  • CFO commentary confirmed a gross margin of 40% for the quarter, consistent with prior quarters, and a consistent focus on improving cost control.

Outlook

  • Koil anticipates benefiting from increased bidding activity in Q1 2025, with projects expected to contribute throughout the year. They are actively pursuing opportunities in key subsea basins, including the Gulf of Mexico, North Sea, and Brazil.
  • The company remains focused on expanding its service offerings to address the operating expenditures of aging infrastructure, including maintenance such as umbilical cable re-termination.
  • Management stated that global subsea tree awards in 2023 and 2024 indicate a healthy market, which aligns with Koil’s strategy to target projects awarded 1 to 1.5 years later.

Financial Results

  • Koil reported Q4 revenue of $6 million, a 14% sequential increase. EBITDA for the quarter was $1 million, reflecting a 16% margin.
  • Full-year 2024 figures included $23 million in revenue, a 48% year-over-year increase, and a net income of $2.6 million, compared to a $1.6 million loss in 2023.
  • Gross margin for 2024 was 38%, up from 32% in 2023, driven by higher fixed-price project activity and improved operational efficiencies.

Q&A

  • Scott Weiss, SEMCO Capital: Asked about the impact of oil prices on operations. Wiik explained that their business is largely unaffected in the short term due to the long planning cycles of subsea projects but acknowledged the potential for delayed maintenance or new projects if oil prices remain low.
  • Weiss inquired about order book visibility and Q1 updates. Wiik noted visibility extends a few quarters but emphasized that backlog reporting is currently not meaningful due to the mix of short- and long-term projects.
  • Peter Michelman, Private Investor: Asked about the effect of tariffs on raw materials. Wiik detailed limited exposure due to vertical integration and contractual protections but admitted residual risk from supplier disruptions and client compliance.
  • Michelman also queried staffing plans for the Brazil facility. Wiik confirmed current headcount at two employees, with future hiring contingent on contract awards.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns over order book transparency and the potential impact of volatile oil prices. Tone was neutral to slightly skeptical.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing growth achievements and strategic positioning. Wiik frequently highlighted the company’s resilience and scalability, stating, “Our clients continue to entrust us with critical project awards.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management demonstrated more confidence in addressing supply chain improvements and international expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased sequentially by 14% in Q4, reversing a 10% decline in Q3 that was attributed to supply chain delays. Margins and EBITDA also showed improvement.
  • Management tone shifted from addressing operational challenges in Q3 to highlighting strategic gains, particularly in international markets and service expansions.
  • Analysts focused more on market risks and operational visibility in the current quarter, reflecting concern over longer-term growth consistency.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified potential revenue fluctuations due to client-driven changes and supply chain risks. They noted efforts to mitigate these risks through improved project management and strategic planning.
  • Analysts highlighted concerns about oil price volatility and its downstream effects on maintenance and new project demand.
  • Tariff-related risks were acknowledged, though partially mitigated through contractual clauses and vertical integration.

Final Takeaway

Koil Energy Solutions closed 2024 with record financial performance, driven by strategic growth in fixed-price projects and international market expansion. While analysts raised concerns about oil price volatility and operational visibility, management highlighted strong bidding activity and plans to leverage a healthy subsea market in 2025. The company is poised for continued growth, supported by a robust cash position, a focus on aging infrastructure solutions, and a scalable business model.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Expand Energy upgraded at Barclays as poised to ramp up cash returns
data.description:
Expand Energy (NASDAQ:EXE) +1.3% in Tuesday's trading as Barclays upgrades shares to Overweight from Equal Weight with a $122 price target, up from $115, citing meaningful operational and marketing synergies from the Southwestern Energy deal as the largest U.S. natural gas producer, with both offensive and defensive characteristics given its ability to grow into demand with its Haynesville position and a strong balance sheet.

Expand's (NASDAQ:EXE) recent decision to ramp to 7.5B cf/day in 2026 designed to maximize free cash flow generation at mid-cycle pricing of $3.50-$4.00/MMBtu was highly accretive to valuation, Barclays analyst Betty Jiang says, noting the company now has one of the highest multiple compressions in its peer group with estimated EV/EBITDX declining more than a full turn to 4.3x in 2026 from 5.5x in 2025 under strip prices.

Meanwhile, the analyst expects free cash flow yield to grow from 8.3% to 12.4% in 2026, seeing the improvement as a catalyst for debt reduction and accelerated buybacks, driving per share
growth.

Jiang also believes Expand (EXE) is poised to materially ramp up its cash returns to investors, expecting the company will generate $2B of free cash flow in 2025 and $2.8B in 2026; based on its current framework, the analyst says this leaves ~$750M available for buybacks in 2025 and up to $1.7B in 2026, after none in 2024.

data.title: 10 S&P stocks defying the downturn as 70% of the index trades below 200-day MA
data.description:
As Wall Street contends with market uncertainty, a deeper look at the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) reveals a sobering trend. Of the 500 companies that make up the index, only 150 are currently trading above their respective 200-day moving averages which is a widely followed technical indicator that helps investors gauge long-term market momentum.

The disparity underscores growing investor caution amid mounting economic headwinds, including ongoing trade tensions and tariff concerns that have weighed heavily on equities in recent weeks.

Despite the market pullback, certain stocks have managed to defy the trend, maintaining strong technical footing and distancing themselves from the broader selloff. Below, Seeking Alpha spotlighted the 10 stocks inside the S&P 500 that currently sit the highest above their 200-day moving averages. 

No. 10: Mckesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK), 18.62% above 200-day MA. 

No. 9: T-Mobile US (NASDAQ:TMUS), 19.64% above 200-day MA.

No. 8: Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX), 20.09% above 200-day MA.

No. 7: EQT Corp (NYSE:EQT), 20.10% above 200-day MA.

No. 6: CenterPoint Energy Inc (NYSE:CNP), 20.52% above 200-day MA.

No. 5:  AT&T (NYSE:T), 21.11% above 200-day MA.

No. 4: Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO), 21.51% above 200-day MA.

No. 3: Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM), 23.22% above 200-day MA.

No. 2: Verisign Inc. (NASDAQ:VRSN), 24.68% above 200-day MA. 

No. 1:  Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR), 62.18% above 200-day MA.

Year-to-date price action: MCK +21.5%, TMUS +21.1%, NFLX +10.6%, EQT +9.7%, CNP +17.6%, T +20.9%, TTWO +16.3%, PM +32.7%, VRSN +20.9%, and PLTR +28.6%.
